title: Daily Briefing — Apr 09
headline: Sharp rally, but flows and sentiment say 'keep your helmet on.'
body: Right, so markets are sprinting today—SPY up +2.5% to 676.01, QQQ +3% to 606.09, and breadth is broad with tech and cyclicals leading. Fear & Greed is still at 32 (Fear), even after this pop, and VIX is elevated but easing (21.07, below SMA20). If you're bullish here, remember: follow the flows—not the headlines. We're just now reclaiming daily 20 MAs across most majors, but weekly 20 MAs remain the mean reversion zone.

Sector rotation? Industrials (XLI, +3.8% to 170.44) and Real Estate (XLRE, +1.7% to 42.44) are in the 'leading' quadrant, with Utilities (XLU) also showing strong momentum. Tech is 'improving' but not leading—note XLK at 141.69 (SMA20: 136.16). Energy (XLE) is tanking -3.5% and flashing 'elevated' sector risk, so don't chase those falling knives. Financials (XLF) are 'improving,' but they're not out of the woods yet, losing key SMA200 recently. Watch the canary.

Contrarian angle: Retail feels hesitant with Fear & Greed still in 'Fear,' but we've exited extreme fear territory (was <20 last week). This is usually the hunting ground for value, but the sheer speed of this rally means patience and reactions beat predictions. Don't ignore the VIX regime—still 'elevated.' If it's in the press, it's in the price. Keep it tight above the 20 MA—less trades, more wins.
callout_body:
SPY reclaiming 20 MA (656.68) and approaching weekly mean reversion zone (673.54).
XLK (Tech) above 20 MA, next resistance around SMA50 (139.06).
XLE (Energy) breaking down, SMA20 at 59.36 now resistance.
XLF (Financials) must hold SMA200 at 52.27.
VIX holding above 20, volatility not fully gone.
Fear & Greed staying below 40 = still room for tactical longs, but not a green-light rally.
action_items: Monitor SPY, QQQ, XLK daily closes—stay long only if above 20 MA.
Avoid Energy sector (XLE, SM) until weakness reverses; watch Financials (XLF) for breakdown/rebound at 52.27.
closing_quote: If bonds freak out, everything breaks. Patience—react, don't predict.
